summary: Upbit clarified that it has only expressed interest in possibly joining the OpenStandard (OUSD) ecosystem at a later date. The statement comes as several South Korean companies are distancing themselves from the OUSD initiative.

title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
content: Just for kicks, I took a look at Jersey Mike's IPO documents. Surely a sandwich shop would have no need to mention AI. But lo and behold.
summary: The author examined the IPO filing of sandwich chain Jersey Mike’s and discovered that, despite its unrelated industry, the document references artificial intelligence. This odd inclusion is used to illustrate how pervasive and exaggerated AI hype has become across various sectors.

title: Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
content: Something stinks in California’s climate policies. Years ago, the state set up a system that pays cattle farmers across the country to turn the methane emitted from cattle manure into natural gas, encouraging the dairy sector to produce a gas we burn instead of one that just pollutes the air. It’s become wildly popular because…
summary: California's climate program pays dairy farmers to capture methane from manure and convert it into natural gas. The article argues that the accounting for the resulting carbon credits is flawed, questioning the environmental benefits of the scheme.

title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
content: Ben Guez has "a bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs," thanks to an automated script he set up using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram trials.
summary: Ben Guez created an automated script using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram experiments to send messages to potential international partners, essentially using AI to aid his dating efforts.

title: Builders Stage agenda revealed: Practical strategies for scaling startups at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
content: The Builders Stage is returning to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, bringing together 10,000+ founders, startup operators, and investors for practical conversations. and Q&A on what it takes to build and scale successful companies. Register now to save up to $330.
summary: The Builders Stage is returning to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, bringing together more than 10,000 founders, startup operators, and investors for practical conversations and Q&A on building and scaling successful companies. Registrants can now sign up to receive a discount of up to $330.

title: US law enforcement group drops opposition to CLARITY Act: Report
content: The Major County Sheriffs of America said it still wants the CLARITY Act amended to give local law enforcement more resources to investigate illicit finance cases.
summary: The Major County Sheriffs of America (MCSA) has reportedly ended its opposition to the CLARITY Act, a legislative effort aimed at combating illicit finance. Despite dropping opposition, the MCSA still seeks amendments to the Act, specifically requesting more resources for local law enforcement to investigate such cases.

title: A device that revives eyeballs from dead donors could make eye transplants possible
content: It’s not easy to transplant a whole human eye. The surgery is difficult. And the eyes themselves start to degenerate as soon as they’ve left the body. When surgeons attempted it a few years ago, the newly transplanted eye wasn’t able to see. But researchers believe they might have a solution: a device that maintains…
summary: Researchers have developed a device that can keep eyeballs from deceased donors alive, preventing degeneration that typically occurs after removal. This breakthrough could enable successful whole-eye transplants, a procedure that has previously failed due to the eye's rapid loss of function post-mortem.

title: The Download: a smoking “endgame” and a new Elizabeth Bear story
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work. I’m supporting it anyway. —Jessica Hamzelou As the parent of two little girls, I often think about how their childhood is different from…
summary: The Download newsletter edition covers a range of technology topics, including skepticism about the UK's generational tobacco ban and mentions a new story by author Elizabeth Bear.

title: The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work. I’m supporting it anyway.
content: As the parent of two little girls, I often think about how their childhood is different from mine. The seven-year-old is learning about AI at school. The five-year-old is given internet-based homework every week. And they are both absolutely repulsed by the idea of smoking. That was not the prevailing sentiment when I was young.…
summary: The author, a parent of two young girls, reflects on how their generation is exposed to AI education and digital homework, while being strongly opposed to smoking. Despite doubts about its effectiveness, the author supports the UK's generational tobacco ban.

title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
content: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.
summary: The article provides an overview of several alternative web browsers that aim to compete with Chrome and Safari, highlighting their features and benefits.

title: Trump asked Musk for SpaceX stock to seed US kids’ savings accounts, report says
content: Sources suggest Musk may be mulling big donation to Trump Accounts.
summary: According to sources, former President Trump approached Elon Musk about a large donation of SpaceX stock to fund savings accounts for American children. The report suggests Musk is considering a sizable contribution to the Trump-backed initiative.
